claims.claim: deepgram-ebook L1238-1244: output filtering verifies model output before TTS; only validated text reaches synthesis; suppress/rewrite before synthesis
claims.found: L1238 'Output Filtering: Controlling What Is Spoken'; L1239-1241 'verifies model output before it is converted to speech. Because spoken responses cannot be retracted, only validated text should ever reach the synthesis stage.'; L1242-1244 'Run each response through a post-generation filter... suppress or rewrite the response before synthesis.'

claims.claim: Together AI rule: guardrails after LLM generation, before TTS because 'you can't take back spoken words' -> catch violations before TTS invoked
claims.found: together-ai md L42 verbatim: 'guardrails after LLM generation, before TTS - because "you can't take back spoken words" -> must catch violations before TTS is invoked.' Raw json: 'You can't take back things that are spoken... catching all of those before the TTS model gets invoked is certainly important.'

claims.claim: Deepgram Chapter 5 'Content Safety and Guardrails' devotes content to harmful-content moderation
claims.found: Chapter 5 title is 'COMPLIANCE AND GOVERNANCE' (L1087+); 'Content Safety and Guardrails' is a section header at L1166 within that chapter. Note labels it a 'Chapter 5' title - minor imprecision (section vs chapter title) but substance (Ch5 covers safety) is correct.

claims.claim: Deepgram Chapter 9 Common Failure Modes appendix does not list 'agent speaks harmful content'; is about latency, audio quality, context
claims.found: CHAPTER 9: APPENDICES 'Common Failure Modes in Real-Time Voice Agents' (L2023+). Listed modes: Slow Responses, Dead Air, Inaccurate Transcription, Choppy Audio, Echo Loops, Tools Not Triggered, Talks Over User, Premature Interruption, Repetitive Responses, Auth Failures, WebSocket Disconnects, Missing Speakers, Loss of Context. No harmful-content mode.

claims.claim: Grep for guardrail/moderation/safety/etc in LiveKit voice/agent_activity.py, agent_session.py, agent.py returns zero
claims.found: All three files exist; grep -i for guardrail|moderation|safety|content_check|before_tts|can_reply|toxicity returned zero matches across them.

claims.claim: LiveKit evals/judge.py has an evaluation-time safety judge, not an in-pipeline guardrail
claims.found: judge.py:424 'def safety_judge(...)' name='safety', L428/440 'toxic or harmful language' / 'harmful, offensive, disrespectful, or toxic language'. It is an LLM evaluation judge (_evaluate_with_llm), not a pipeline frame processor.

claims.claim: Pipecat processors/ has no safety/guardrail processor between LLM output and TTS input
claims.found: processors dir exists with 42 .py files; grep -i guardrail|moderation|toxicity returned zero matches. Real negative, not a missing-path artifact.

claims.claim: No OSS voice-engine clone implements a pre-TTS guardrail gate today
claims.found: Cross-clone grep for 'guardrail' only hit tests (toxic_proxy.py, test_ipc.py) and ipc/job_proc_lazy_main.py - none in the LLM->TTS hot path. Consistent with the note's conclusion.

summary: All 8 checkable assertions verified against ground truth. Deepgram L1238-1244 quote is exact; Together AI quote matches raw transcript; Chapter 9 failure-mode list omits harmful content as claimed; LiveKit/Pipecat clone-audit negatives confirmed; evals/judge.py safety_judge confirmed as eval-time not in-pipeline. One cosmetic imprecision: 'Content Safety and Guardrails' is a section header within Chapter 5 'Compliance and Governance', not the chapter title - substance unaffected. No WRONG or MISCITED claims. Replaces a stale prior FAIL verdict.
